text: The technique of separation of the posterior components for the release of the transversus abdominal muscle is a new method. The advantages include preservation of neurovascular bundles, significant myofascial medialization, and creation of a well-vascularized plane for sublay mesh placement. Recently, the safety as well as long-term clinical efficacy of the release of the transversus abdominal muscle technique has also been reported.

We present the case of a patient with a peristomal hernia and hypogastric incisional hernia. He was subjected to anterior rectum resection and subsequent terminal colostomy. Bilateral release of the transversus abdominal muscle was the only way to rebuild the wall without tension.

The most frequent scenario for release of the transversus abdominal muscle is large midline defects where a traditional rive stop repair fails to provide re-approximation of the posterior layers a. Endelinea alba reconstruction or create space for sufficient mesh overlap.

The release of the transversus abdominal muscle procedure is a versatile technique that has been shown to work in challenging hernia locations such as subcostal. Subxiphoid, flank, peristomal, and suprapubic.
Minimally invasive release of the transversus abdominal muscle is feasible and guarantees rapid postoperative recovery.
